input.prompt:
I have a new C# MCP server project. I need to implement a tool class that
wraps a REST API using HttpClient. The tools should follow MCP SDK conventions
with proper attributes for LLM discovery. Show me the tool class, Program.cs
with stdio transport, and explain how DI works for MCP tools.

data.content:
I have a new C# MCP server project. I need to implement a tool class that
wraps a REST API using HttpClient. The tools should follow MCP SDK conventions
with proper attributes for LLM discovery. Show me the tool class, Program.cs
with stdio transport, and explain how DI works for MCP tools.

<current_datetime>2026-05-08T00:51:04.577Z</current_datetime>

I have a new C# MCP server project. I need to implement a tool class that
wraps a REST API using HttpClient. The tools should follow MCP SDK conventions
with proper attributes for LLM discovery. Show me the tool class, Program.cs
with stdio transport, and explain how DI works for MCP tools.

input.initialPrompt:
I have a new C# MCP server project. I need to implement a tool class that
wraps a REST API using HttpClient. The tools should follow MCP SDK conventions
with proper attributes for LLM discovery. Show me the tool class, Program.cs
with stdio transport, and explain how DI works for MCP tools.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command: cd /tmp/sv-e97dd474923244df9b67e063ab1c89a6 && mkdir -p McpServer && cd McpServer && dotnet new console --no-restore -q && dotnet add package ModelContextProtocol --prerelease --no-restore && dotnet add package Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection --no-restore && dotnet add package Microsoft.Extensions.Http --no-restore && dotnet restore -q 2>&1 | tail -5
arguments.description: Create MCP server project with dependencies

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Create MCP server project with dependencies

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to create a C# MCP (Model Context Protocol) server project with:
1. A tool class wrapping a REST API using HttpClient
2. Program.cs with stdio transport
3. An explanation of how DI (Dependency Injection) works for MCP tools

Let me provide a complete example. I'll create the files in their current working directory.
